summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring a major public hearing on zoning case ZC-2025-006 for the Eagles Nest Golf Course redevelopment into a mixed-use community with commercial, residential, apartments, and townhomes. The board approved the zoning change with conditions, accepted an ODOT safety sign grant of $58,543.81, scheduled a strategic planning workshop, accepted a heritage tree from the Clermont County Park District, and passed a resolution to pursue changing the township's postal address designation with USPS.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for September 10-23, 2025

Approved zoning case ZC-2025-006 (Eagles Nest Golf Course rezoning to PD Mixed Use) with conditions including traffic safety measures, speed limits, road design standards, ODOT recommendations compliance, phased development schedule, and matching township street signs
Scheduled special Board of Trustees strategic planning workshop for Thursday, November 13 from noon to 6 PM
Accepted ODOT Township Safety Sign Grant up to $58,543.81
Accepted Clermont County Park District heritage white oak tree for America 250 celebration
Accepted donation of $345 in meal gift cards from TQL for police officers
Approved revised Police Policy 504 (Outside and Extra Duty Employment) raising cruiser rate from $10 to $15 per hour
Approved Resolution R-023-2025 to pursue changing USPS postal address designation to Goshen
action_items: Director Meadows to send list of roads included in ODOT sign grant to trustees
Director Meadows to prepare site at Jones House for heritage white oak tree planting (remove/grind existing stump)
Staff to coordinate with USPS on postal address/boundary change for Goshen Township
Director Langenheim/staff to work with Drees on development agreement incorporating approved stipulations
Administrator Rose to organize strategic planning workshop for November 13
Director Meadows to look into costs of flashing warning signs for stop sign intersections
Drees/developer to comply with ODOT traffic study recommendations including likely traffic light at main entrance
key_amounts: $58,543.81 - ODOT Township Safety Sign Grant accepted
$345 - TQL meal gift card donation for police officers ($15 per officer x 23 officers)
$150 million - referenced as scale of Drees' Montgomery Quarters mixed-use development experience
$1,200 to $1,900/month - expected apartment rents at Eagles Nest development
$325,000 to $419,000 - expected townhome prices at Eagles Nest development
$15/hour - new cruiser rate for outside/extra duty employment (increased from $10/hour)
93 sixty-foot lots, 84 fifty-two-foot lots, ~252 apartments, 54 Type 1 townhomes, 72 Type 2 townhomes proposed
163 acres total development, ~28 acres commercial, 46+ acres open space
notable_topics: Eagles Nest Golf Course rezoning to mixed-use development (ZC-2025-006) - commercial, residential, apartments, townhomes
ODOT traffic study and road improvements for State Route 28 corridor
Sentencing conclusion in Evelyn Kammerer child death case (19-24 years and 5 years for defendants)
ODOT Township Safety Sign Grant for multiple township roads
Strategic planning workshop scheduling for next 5-year plan
Clermont County Park District heritage white oak tree for America 250
USPS postal address designation change resolution for Goshen Township
Police policy revision for outside duty employment cruiser rates
Turn arrow installation at Route 28 and Goshen Road intersection
Grocery store anchor tenant negotiations (unnamed national chain)
School capacity concerns related to new development growth
Flock camera requests for development egress points
notes: Transcript had significant portions of garbled/repeated text ('Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.') likely due to automated transcription errors. The Eagles Nest development is on the former Eagles Nest Golf Course at 1540 State Route 28. The developer is working with an unnamed national grocery store chain as anchor tenant. Cincinnati United soccer complex is an adjacent neighbor. The apartment count was reduced from 300 to 252 and lot widths were increased (40ft to 52ft and 50ft to 60ft) resulting in about 50 fewer single-family homes since earlier plans. Trustee Hodges noted he is 70 years old, born and raised in Goshen, graduated from Goshen High School in 1973. ODOT denied a speed reduction/traffic signal request east of Goshen on Route 28.
title: BOT 9-23-25
